name: Berry Liqueur
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces Raspberries, or any other berry
1 1/2 cups Vodka, or 1 cup vodka and 1/2 cup brandy
recipeInstructions:
1. Make sugar syrup by boiling 1 cup sugar with 1/2 cup water until the sugar is thoroughly dissolved.
2. Allow the mixture to cool before using.
3. Add juice and berries to alcohol in a jar or container.
4. Stir well and steep for one week, ensuring the mixture remains covered.
5. Strain the liquid into a clean container, pressing on the solids to extract as much flavor as possible.
6. Crush the berries through a fine mesh strainer to remove any large pieces of fruit.
7. Taste the liqueur and adjust sweetness by adding more sugar syrup if needed.
8. Store the finished liqueur in an airtight container in the refrigerator.
Many frozen fruits are already heavily pre-sugared, so you may not need additional sugar syrup. If using unsugared fruits without syrup, treat them as fresh fruit but reduce the amount of water when making the sugar syrup because of the higher water content in the frozen fruit. The final yield will be about 2 cups.
